description: This PRD addresses gaps in the UI layer where domain models exist but lack visibility or management interfaces. The backend models (main PRD US-001 through US-032) and read-only views (UI PRD US-001 through US-016) are implemented. This PRD adds admin/management capabilities, debug views, and fixes to existing UI gaps.

userStories.title: Organization Switcher
userStories.description: As an authenticated user with access to multiple organizations, I want an organization switcher so that all scoped data updates to reflect the selected org.
acceptanceCriteria: Organization switcher dropdown in the sidebar showing the user's organizations
Clicking an organization sets it as the user's current organization
Current organization persisted in session (or on user model)
All org-scoped pages (dashboard, projects, escalations, teams) reflect the selected organization
After switching, user is redirected to dashboard with new org context
Feature test: user switches org, dashboard shows data for the new org

userStories.title: Fix Project Dashboard Clickability Gaps
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want all summary cards and list items on the project dashboard to be clickable so that I can navigate to the relevant detail pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Repos count card links to repos list page (US-003)
Active story rows link to story detail page
Milestone rows link to milestone detail page (US-006)
Feature test: all dashboard cards and list items have correct links

userStories.title: Repos List
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to see a list of repositories for a project so that I can understand what codebases are managed.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/repos
Table showing: name, URL, primary language, default branch, tags
Link from each repo to repo detail page
Link/button to create a new repo
Feature test: repos list displays project repos

userStories.title: Repo CRUD
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to create, edit, and delete repositories for a project so that the system knows which codebases agents work on.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/repos/create -- form to add a new repo

Route: GET /projects/{project}/repos/{repo} -- repo detail page showing name, URL, primary language, default branch, tags, and counts of branches, worktrees, and pull requests (counts link to respective list pages)
Route: GET /projects/{project}/repos/{repo}/edit -- form to edit repo
Delete action with confirmation
Form fields: name (required), URL (required), primary language, default branch (default: main), tags (comma-separated or multi-input)
Validation via Form Request
Feature test: create, read, update, delete repos

userStories.title: Milestones List
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to see a list of milestones for a project so that I can track delivery targets and progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/milestones
Table showing: title, status, due date, story count, bug count
Filter by status (open, active, closed)
Link from each milestone to milestone detail page
Link/button to create a new milestone
Feature test: milestones list displays project milestones

userStories.title: Milestone CRUD
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to create, edit, and delete milestones for a project so that work items can be scheduled against delivery targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/milestones/create -- form to add a new milestone
Route: GET /projects/{project}/milestones/{milestone} -- milestone detail page showing title, description, status, due date, and lists of assigned stories and bugs with their statuses
Route: GET /projects/{project}/milestones/{milestone}/edit -- form to edit milestone
Delete action with confirmation (only when no stories/bugs assigned)
Form fields: title (required), description, status, due date
Validation via Form Request
Feature test: create, read, update, delete milestones

userStories.title: Labels Management
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to manage labels for a project so that stories and bugs can be categorized.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/labels
List of labels showing: name, color, count of attached stories, count of attached bugs
Inline create form (name + color picker) for adding new labels
Inline edit for existing labels
Delete action with confirmation
Feature test: create, list, edit, delete labels

userStories.title: Label Attachment on Story and Bug Detail
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to attach and detach labels on stories and bugs so that work items are categorized.
acceptanceCriteria: Labels section on story detail page showing attached labels
Dropdown or autocomplete to attach a label from the project's labels
Action to detach a label
Same functionality on bug detail page
Feature test: attach and detach labels on stories and bugs

userStories.title: Milestone Detail
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view a milestone's details and progress so that I can understand delivery status.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/milestones/{milestone}
Header: title, status, due date, description
Progress summary: total stories, completed stories, total bugs, fixed bugs
List of assigned stories with status
List of assigned bugs with status
Feature test: milestone detail displays all sections

userStories.title: Agent Types List and Detail
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to view and manage agent types so that I can configure the roles available to agents.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /agent-types
List of agent types showing: name, slug, description, agent count
Route: GET /agent-types/{agentType} -- detail page showing description, default capabilities, and list of agents of this type
Create and edit forms for agent types
New "Platform" sidebar navigation group with Agent Types item
Feature test: list, detail, create, edit agent types

userStories.title: Agent Detail
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to view an agent's details so that I can understand its configuration, current assignments, and recent activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /agents/{agent}
Header: name, type, team, model, status, confidence threshold
Capabilities and tools lists
Currently assigned stories, bugs, and ops requests
Recent action logs for this agent (last 20)
Link from teams page agent rows to this detail page
Feature test: agent detail displays all sections

userStories.title: Agent CRUD
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to create, edit, and delete agents so that I can configure the agent workforce.
acceptanceCriteria: Create form accessible from teams page or agent types detail page
Form fields: name (required), agent type (select), team (select, scoped to org), model (string), confidence threshold (0-1), tools (multi-input), capabilities (multi-input), status
Edit form pre-populated with current values
Delete action with confirmation
Validation via Form Request
Feature test: create, edit, delete agents

userStories.title: Team CRUD
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to create, edit, and delete teams so that agents can be organized by function.
acceptanceCriteria: Create form accessible from teams list page
Form fields: name (required), organization (pre-filled from current org context)
Edit form for renaming a team
Manage project assignments: add/remove projects assigned to the team
Delete action with confirmation (only when no agents assigned)
Feature test: create, edit, delete teams, manage project assignments

userStories.title: Task and Subtask Management on Story Detail
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view tasks and subtasks on a story with the ability to reorder them and update their status so that implementation progress is tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: Tasks section on story detail shows tasks ordered by position
Each task shows: title, type, status, subtask count
Subtasks expandable under each task, ordered by position
Status update action on tasks and subtasks (select from valid statuses)
Drag-to-reorder or move up/down buttons for tasks and subtasks
Feature test: update task status, reorder tasks

userStories.title: Dependency Management on Story and Bug Detail
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to add and remove blocked-by dependencies on stories and bugs so that work sequencing is correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Dependencies section on story detail shows "blocked by" items with type (story/bug) and status
Search/select to add a new dependency (search stories and bugs in same project)
Remove dependency action
Same functionality on bug detail page
Visual indicator when a dependency is resolved vs unresolved
Feature test: add and remove dependencies on stories and bugs

userStories.title: Attachments on Story and Bug Detail
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view, upload, and delete attachments on stories and bugs so that supporting artifacts are accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: Attachments section on story and bug detail pages
Each attachment shows: filename, mime type, size, upload date
Upload action with file picker
Download link for each attachment
Delete action with confirmation
Feature test: upload, list, download, delete attachments

userStories.title: Branches List (Read-only Debug View)
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view branches for a repo so that I can understand what code work is in progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/repos/{repo}/branches
Table showing: branch name, base branch, linked work item (type + title), created at
Work item links navigate to the relevant story/bug/ops-request detail page
Accessible from repo detail page
Feature test: branches list displays repo branches

userStories.title: Worktrees List (Read-only Debug View)
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view worktrees for a repo so that I can debug agent workspace state and detect stale work.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/repos/{repo}/worktrees
Table showing: path, status (active/interrupted/stale), linked work item, branch, last activity, interrupted at, interrupted reason
Status color coding: active=green, interrupted=amber, stale=red
Work item links navigate to the relevant detail page
Accessible from repo detail page
Feature test: worktrees list displays repo worktrees

userStories.title: Change Sets and Pull Requests Detail (Read-only Debug View)
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view change set details including all pull requests and their reviews so that I can understand the full code review pipeline for a work item.
acceptanceCriteria: Expand change set section on story/bug/ops-request detail pages to show full detail
Each change set shows: status, summary, list of pull requests
Each pull request shows: title, status, repo, branch, author agent, external URL link, list of reviews
Each review shows: reviewer agent, status, body excerpt, submitted at
Feature test: change set detail displays PRs and reviews

userStories.title: Action Log Page (Project Scope)
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view the action log for a project so that I can audit agent activity and debug decision-making.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/action-logs
Table showing: timestamp, agent name, action, work item (type + title), reasoning excerpt
Filter by agent, action type, work item type (story/task/bug/ops-request)
Sort by timestamp (default: newest first)
Pagination
Expandable reasoning text (truncated in table, full on click/expand)
Work item links navigate to the relevant detail page
Accessible from project dashboard sidebar or navigation
Feature test: action logs display with filters

userStories.title: Action Log Page (Global / Org Scope)
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to view a global action log across all projects in my organization so that I can monitor agent activity org-wide.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /action-logs
Same table format as project-scoped action log but includes project column
Filter by project, agent, action type, work item type
Sort by timestamp (default: newest first)
Pagination
Sidebar navigation item under Platform group
Feature test: global action log displays org-scoped data with filters

userStories.title: Runbook Detail (Read-only Debug View)
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to view a runbook's full details and step-by-step execution status so that I can review operational procedures and their outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: GET /projects/{project}/ops-requests/{opsRequest}/runbook
Header: title, status, description
Ordered list of steps showing: position, instruction, status (pending/executing/completed/failed/skipped), executed by, executed at
Step status color coding: completed=green, failed=red, executing=blue, skipped=zinc, pending=amber
Accessible from ops request detail page as a link (when runbook exists)
Feature test: runbook detail displays steps with status

userStories.title: Project Sub-navigation
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want contextual sub-navigation when viewing a project so that I can quickly switch between project sections without returning to the project dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: When on any project page, a sub-navigation bar or tabs appears below the breadcrumbs
Sub-nav items: Overview, Epics, Stories, Bugs, Ops Requests, Repos, Milestones, Labels, Action Logs
Current section highlighted
Sub-nav only visible on project-scoped pages
Feature test: sub-navigation renders with correct active state
